questions.question: Define consensus in the context of distributed systems and explain its importance in blockchain.
keyPoints: Process of reaching agreement among distributed nodes
Ensures all nodes have the same view of the ledger
Required because there is no central authority
Must handle node failures and network delays
Foundation for trust in decentralized systems
questions.modelAnswer: Consensus in distributed systems is the process by which multiple nodes in a network agree on a single data value or state. In blockchain, consensus ensures all participants agree on the order and validity of transactions without requiring a central authority. This is crucial because in decentralized networks, nodes may receive transactions in different orders, some may be offline, and some may be malicious. Consensus provides the mechanism to achieve a consistent view across all honest nodes.

questions.question: Explain the four key properties of consensus protocols: Agreement, Validity, Termination, and Integrity.
keyPoints: Agreement: All non-faulty nodes decide on the same value
Validity: Output must be a value proposed by some node
Termination: All nodes must eventually reach a decision
Integrity: Decision can only be on proposed values
Explain importance of each property

questions.question: Compare centralized database consistency with blockchain consensus mechanisms.
keyPoints: Trust model differences
Failure handling approaches
Consistency guarantees
Performance trade-offs
Use cases for each

questions.question: Explain the CAP theorem and how blockchain systems navigate its constraints.
keyPoints: Define Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance
Explain the impossibility of achieving all three
How Bitcoin prioritizes partition tolerance
Trade-offs between finality and liveness
Different choices by different blockchains
